channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Moniepoint partners ALU to train tech leaders
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Moniepoint Inc. partners with African Leadership University to launch an executive development program, strengthening its leadership pipeline and supportin
Read More: https://punchng.com/moniepoint-partners-alu-to-train-tech-leaders/

item.description:
Nigerian PR professionals are advocating for increased boardroom influence, moving beyond storytelling to strategic advisory roles. Discover how they’re sh
Read More: https://punchng.com/pr-practitioners-push-for-greater-boardroom-influence/

item.title: Alarming Malami: Goons of graft are many
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Former AGF Abubakar Malami faces EFCC charges of money laundering and conspiracy involving billions. Discover the allegations and ongoing legal battles.
Read More: https://punchng.com/alarming-malami-goons-of-graft-are-many/

item.title: NBA general election: Far-reaching recommendations and prognosis for the future
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Explore the controversies of the 2026 NBA Election and discover far-reaching recommendations to ensure credible and transparent future NBA elections.
Read More: https://punchng.com/nba-general-election-far-reaching-recommendations-and-prognosis-for-the-future/

item.title: Vigilant resident, swift Police response aid recovery of pistol, car

item.description:
Gombe State Police Command recovered a Beretta pistol and a stolen Toyota Camry thanks to a vigilant resident’s tip-off and swift police action. Public coo
Read More: https://punchng.com/vigilant-resident-swift-police-response-aid-recovery-of-pistol-car/

President Tinubu’s administration is increasingly relying on World Bank loans to fund social programs, aiming to ease reform pains and support vulnerable N

item.description:
PDP Reps candidate Zaraddeen Aminu flags off a 2km road project in Bauchi, promising more development if elected. Learn about his commitment to infrastruct

item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
An Enugu Air aircraft veered off the runway at Benin Airport, prompting an NSIB probe and flight suspensions. All 68 on board are safe after the Enugu Air

item.title: Subsidised imports threaten Nigeria’s agricultural growth – FG
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Subsidised imports and global trade distortions threaten Nigeria’s agricultural growth, says Minister Bagudu. Discover how this impacts food security and e
Read More: https://punchng.com/subsidised-imports-threaten-nigerias-agricultural-growth-fg/

item.title: Trump threatens Iran and Houthi rebels with 'major military punishment'
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Iran and the United States threatened to intensify attacks on Thursday after Yemen's Houthi rebels struck Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea, driving Brent crude above $100 a barrel and fuelling fears of a widening regional conflict and renewed pressure on the global economy.

item.title: Scheffler bounces back from Open putting woes with strong start in Minnesota
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Scottie Scheffler bounced back from his poor performance with his putter at last week's Open as he returned to PGA Tour action on Thursday to card a six-under par 65 at the 3M Open.

item.title: PFN urges Tinubu to reject anti-preaching bill
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Body says proposed law is unnecessary, targeted at Christians By Olayinka Ajayi LAGOS — The Pentecostal Fellowship of Nigeria (PFN) has urged President Bola Tinubu not to assent to the anti-preaching and hawking bill currently before the National Assembly, describing it as unnecessary and unrelated

item.title: Osun Election: I didn’t own house while serving as Osun governor — Aregbesola
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Shina Abubakar, Osogbo OSOGBO — National Secretary of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Rauf Aregbesola, has claimed that he did not own a personal house throughout his eight years as governor of Osun State. Aregbesola made the claim while addressing supporters in a video that circulated onl

item.title: Igboho vows to flush out bandits from Kwara forests
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Launches Iru Ekun Security Network, says outfit will support security agencies By Demola Akinyemi, Ilorin ILORIN — Yoruba Nation activist, Sunday Adeyemo, popularly known as Sunday Igboho, has vowed that his Iru Ekun Security Network will reclaim forests in Kwara State from bandits and other crimina

item.title: Most FDA advisers say peptides like BPC-157 and TB-500 should be made available in the U.S. Here’s what happens next.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The FDA is deciding whether seven peptides can be legally compounded, potentially opening the door to broader use in Americans.

item.title: Lawan applauds Tinubu’s expansion of army to 12 Divisions
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Commends recruitment of 28,000 personnel, says move will improve response to security threats By Henry Umoru ABUJA — Former Senate President and Chairman of the Senate Committee on Defence, Senator Ahmad Lawan (APC, Yobe North), has commended President Bola Tinubu for expanding the Nigerian Army to

item.title: Nigerian onion traders seek Ghana’s intervention over detention of trucks
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Cynthia AloThe Regional Observatory of Onion in West and Central Africa, ORO/WCA, has appealed to the Government of Ghana to urgently intervene in the continued detention of five trucks loaded with Nigerian onions at Kotoku Market, warning that the development is causing heavy losses on farmers,

item.title: Gold Holds Decline as Surging Energy Costs Raise Rate-Hike Bets
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Gold held a retreat as the widening conflict in the Middle East pressured energy prices and increased expectations the US Federal Reserve will tighten monetary policy to contain inflation.

item.title: MSSN hails court judgment on right of Muslim female students to wear hijab in public schools in Oyo
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Muslim Students’ Society of Nigeria (MSSN), Oyo State Area Unit, has hailed what it described as a landmark judgment delivered by a high court sitting in Ibadan, granting constitutional right to Muslim female students to wear Islamic hijab in government-owned schools across Oyo State. According

item.title: Sudan: 80 Sudanese Leave Uganda On Voluntary Return Flight
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Dabanga] Amsterdam / Kampala -- The 11th voluntary return flight carrying Sudanese refugees from Uganda to Sudan departed on Thursday with 80 returnees on board. The flight travelled to Port Sudan Airport aboard a Tarco Aviation aircraft and was sponsored by Sudan's General Intelligence Service.

item.title: Sudan: 'U.S. Congress Should Pass New Bills On Sudan' - Human Rights Watch
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Dabanga] Washington -- The US Congress is currently considering two bills on Sudan: the Preventing External Aggression and Conflict Escalation (PEACE) in Sudan Act of 2026 in the Senate and US Engagement in Sudanese Peace Act in the House of Representatives. Human Rights Watch (HRW) asserts that "b

item.title: New PM cuts business rates for UK pubs in latest cost-of-living measure
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: British Prime Minister Andy Burnham announced a 20% cut to business rates for pubs, clubs and live music venues in England on Thursday, his third major cost-of-living measure in three days as the new Labour leader seeks to revive the economy and support struggling high streets.

item.title: Mbah advocates experiential learning, unveils Enugu’s human dev model at UNN convocation lecture
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Governor Peter Mbah of Enugu State has called for a fundamental overhaul of Nigeria’s education system, urging schools to focus on producing problem-solvers and innovators rather than graduates armed only with certificates. The post Mbah advocates experiential learning, unveils Enugu’s human dev mod

item.title: Nasarawa lawmaker raises alarm over killing of 32 persons in constituency
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Edwin Philip, Lafia A member of the Nasarawa State House of Assembly representing Udege/Loko Constituency, Hon. Onarigu Onah Kana, has raised the alarm over renewed violence in his constituency, alleging that at least 32 people have been killed in the last three months. Speaking under a matter of

item.title: Group faults Obi’s single-term proposal, says it’s detrimental to S’East
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Imo State chapter of the Renewed Hope Ambassadors (RHA) has rejected a reported agreement that would commit Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC) presidential candidate, Mr. Peter Obi, to a single four-year term if elected in 2027, describing it as an attempt to reduce “Igbo presidential aspiration to a

item.title: When the judiciary becomes ultimate threat to democracy
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The judiciary is often described as the last hope of the common man. Whenever disputes arise, citizens turn to the courts for redress. When the powerful seeks to oppress the weak, the latter finds refuge in the temple of justice. This enduring faith in the judiciary rests on the expectation that jud

item.title: Collapsed Benin–Sapele–Warri Road: End cycle of palliative repairs, Ugolor urges Oborevwori
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Demands cost of governor’s ordered emergency repairs By Egufe Yafugborhi ASABA — Civil society leader, Rev. David Ugolor, has urged Delta State Governor, Sheriff Oborevwori, to engage the Federal Government and neighbouring Edo State to end decades of temporary repairs on the collapsed Benin–S

item.title: NDIC promotes financial literacy, deposit protection in Ebonyi
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Jeff Agbodo, Abakaliki The Nigeria Deposit Insurance Corporation (NDIC) has organised a financial literacy campaign for students in Ebonyi State to enhance their knowledge of personal finance and create awareness about the protection of bank deposits. The awareness programme was held for staff an

item.title: Fields medal honours four mathematicians as AI reshapes the profession
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The International Mathematical Union awarded the prestigious Fields Medal to four mathematicians on Thursday, including only the third woman to receive the honour, as researchers weighed the growing impact of artificial intelligence on a field increasingly being reshaped by the technology.

item.title: How the Houthi Red Sea blockade will impact the world
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The Houthis' blockade of Bab al-Mandeb threatens a vital global trade route.

item.title: TMC reaffirms commitment to spiritual growth, says education central in Islam
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: THE Muslim Congress (TMC), Oyo State chapter, has reaffirmed its commitment to educational development and spiritual growth with the successful organisation of its 2026 Qur'an Competition and Spelling Bee for secondary school students across the state. The post TMC reaffirms commitment to spiritual

item.title: Police Recover Stolen Hilux, Arrest Two Suspected Vehicle Thieves
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The police have recovered a stolen 2022 Toyota Hilux and arrested two suspected members of a vehicle theft syndicate after an intelligence-led operation across Plateau and Bauchi states. The Plateau State Police Command said the Hilux, with registration number 18D-35-FG, was stolen from its owner&#8

item.title: Team Nigeria Shines At Spectacular Glasgow 2026 Opening Ceremony, Sets Sights On Commonwealth Glory
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The 23rd Commonwealth Games officially began in spectacular fashion on Thursday night as Team Nigeria made a memorable entrance during a dazzling Opening Ceremony at Glasgow’s OVO Hydro Arena, setting the stage for what the National Sports Commission (NSC) hopes will be another successful campaign.

item.title: CLO Price Swings Dictated by Tokyo, Wharton-Led Study Shows
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The $1.3 trillion US CLO market is taking its cues from Tokyo as much as Wall Street.

item.title: EFCC Arraigns Man Over Alleged N3.8m Property Fraud
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) has arraigned Alhaji Muhammad Talake before the Borno State High Court in Maiduguri over an alleged N3.8 million property fraud. Talake appeared before Justice Aisha Kumaliya on Thursday on a two-count charge of obtaining by false pretence and crim

item.title: NSCDC Arrests Six Over Abuja Pipeline Vandalism, Fuel Theft
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Nigeria Security and Civil Defence Corps (NSCDC), Federal Capital Territory (FCT) Command, has arrested six suspected pipeline vandals accused of puncturing Nigerian National Petroleum Company (NNPC) pipelines and stealing Premium Motor Spirit (PMS) in Gwagwalada Area Council. The suspects were

item.title: Arab and Muslim countries condemn Israeli storming of Al-Aqsa Mosque
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Israeli settlers' storming of Islam's holy site fuels 'hatred' and obstructs lasting peace, joint statement says.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Former President Goodluck Ebele Jonathan, has expressed his sense of fulfilment at the enactment of the Nigerian Oil and Gas Industry Content Development (NOGICD) Act, 2010, and the many benefits that have accrued to Nigeria at the national and community levels since the emergence of the Nigerian Co

item.title: Murray returns as Draper's coach for Mubadala DC Open
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Andy Murray and Jack Draper appear to be working together once again after the 24-year-old was forced to miss out on Wimbledon earlier this month.

item.title: Indian activist ends 26-day hunger strike, though student protests continue
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike on Friday after talks with government ministers, but student-led protests over leaked medical entrance exam papers continued, with demonstrators demanding the education minister's resignation and wider reforms.

item.title: CJC Resolves 70 Land Disputes In Imo
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Imo State Community Justice Centre (CJC) in Owerri, on Thursday said they have resolved 70 land disputes, out of the 185 cases handled during its one year pilot phase. This is even as the Hague Institute for Innovation of Law (HIIL), has announced that the centre will be formally handed over to

item.title: 'Good luck to Dirk...': Littler dazzles in Blackpool as 'frightening' form continues
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: If a player hit a nine-darter on the World Matchplay stage, that would usually go down as their highlight. That is just the tip of the iceberg for Luke Littler in 2026.

item.title: Mobileye CEO Amnon Shashua to step aside as company pushes into robotaxis, robotics
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Shashua has been invited to take the chairman of the board seat.

item.title: EFCC Returns N108m Recovered From Employees To Company
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) has handed over N108 million recovered during an investigation into an alleged fraud involving two former employees of Greenwich Planet Sun King Nigeria Limited. The funds were presented to the company in bank drafts on Wednesday by the EFCC’

item.title: Police Arrest Two Over N32m Goods Diversion
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Oyo State Police Command has arrested two suspected members of a syndicate and recovered a truck loaded with 570 bags of guinea corn valued at about N32m. The suspects, Olawale Kareem, 40, and Ogunbiyi Monday, 46, were arrested by operatives of the Command Monitoring Unit, Oyo Operational Base.

item.title: ‘I deceived nobody,’ Obasanjo fires back at Osoba over 2003 political row
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Obasanjo said Osoba was not privy to several political consultations that preceded his decision to contest the presidency after his release from prison.

item.title: Tinubu Approves Expansion Of Nigerian Army To 12 Divisions
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu Commander-in-Chief of the Armed Forces of the Federal Republic of Nigeria, has approved the expansion of the Nigerian Army’s structure from eight to twelve divisions a landmark move to enhance the country’s security architecture and improve the operational effectiveness o

item.title: Trump says frozen Iranian assets will pay for damage to shipping
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: President Donald Trump said Thursday that the US would begin using frozen Iranian assets to pay for ships and cargo damaged by strikes in and around the Gulf. The post Trump says frozen Iranian assets will pay for damage to shipping appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.dc:creator: Vanguard Sports
item.description: Modric joined the Italian heavyweights in 2025, scoring two goals in 37 matches last season. The post Modric signs new AC Milan deal appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Trive-Backed Lyntris Files For US IPO, Joining Defense Rush
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Lyntris Inc., a defense technology firm backed by Trive Capital, filed for a US initial public offering.

item.title: Drivers hail Sanwo-Olu for swift response over abandoned refuse at Obalende Motor Parks
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Both drivers and commuters thanked Sanwo-Olu for saving them from health hazards by giving a marching order to the Lawma workers to clear the refuse at the parks.

item.description: Osun State governor, Ademola Adeleke, has ordered the immediate suspension of all activities of the National Union of Road Transport Workers (NURTW) in motor parks across the state as part of efforts to restore order and strengthen public security. The directive was issued on Thursday after a meetin

item.title: The August stock-market slump is a myth — so why does Wall Street keep repeating it?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: More than 200 years of data show stocks typically gain in August, while market volatility sits well below average.

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Presidency has accused former vice president and presidential candidate of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Atiku Abubakar, of acting in bad faith over his recent petition filed in the United States against President Bola Tinubu. item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Presidency on Thursday criticised former Vice President Atiku Abubakar over his reported move to revive President Bola Tinubu’s decades-old U.S. civil forfeiture case through a Washington-based lobbying firm.   Presidential spokesman Bayo Onanuga, in a statement, describ

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Stocks in Asia were poised to fall after a sharp tech-led selloff on Wall Street, while Brent surged above $100 a barrel for the first time in two months as the escalating Middle East conflict rekindled inflation concerns.

item.title: Stop spiritualising autism, experts urge ahead of 2026 GTCO conference
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: As preparations ramp up for the 16th annual GTCO Autism Conference, stakeholders and behavioural experts are calling for a massive cultural shift in how society treats neurodivergent individuals. Moving away from the elementary stage of mere “awareness,” this year’s convention—them

item.title: TikTok removes 4.8m Nigerian videos in Q1 2026 as AI tightens content policing
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: TikTok removed more than 4.8 million videos from its Nigerian platform in the first quarter of 2026, as the social

item.title: Falz’s “Na Je” Video Takes Social Commentary Straight to the Streets of Northern Nigeria
item.dc:creator: BellaNaija
item.description: Falz drops the official video for "Na Je" off his EP Break Time, featuring Oiza x Meyi and FirstKlaz. Shot entirely in Northern Nigeria, the visuals bring the song's social commentary on survival and economic hardship to life through the everyday rhythms of local life. The post Falz’s “N

item.title: Trump warns of largest strikes on Iran yet as Tehran lashes out across Gulf
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The US president's threat comes after another day of Iranian attacks on Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan.

item.title: EFCC: APC chieftain warned me not to contest against Tinubu in 2027 — Makinde
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Oyo State Governor, Seyi Makinde has disclosed that an APC chieftain had forewarned him not to contest against President Tinubu in 2027... The post EFCC: APC chieftain warned me not to contest against Tinubu in 2027 — Makinde appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: US-Saudi nuclear deal hinges on normalisation with Israel
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: President Trump said a proposed US-Saudi civilian nuclear deal is subject to Saudi Arabia joining the Abraham Accords

item.title: ServiceNow’s stock falls as a new AI threat overshadows earnings beat
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: While ServiceNow’s earnings beat proved that its AI momentum is picking up, the launch of OpenAI Presence reignited fears of enterprise software disruption

item.title: FULL LIST: Names of 68 lawyers elevated to SAN rank

item.description: The Legal Practitioners' Privileges Committee (LPPC) has conferred the prestigious rank of Senior Advocate of Nigeria (SAN) on The post FULL LIST: Names of 68 lawyers elevated to SAN rank appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: PDP Stakeholders Back Turaki Leadership, To Appeal Court Judgment
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) says its Board of Trustees, state chairmen and governorship candidates have unanimously reaffirmed their support for the party's Interim National Working Committee led by Kabiru Tanimu Turaki (SAN), following last week's Federal High Court judgment in Abuja. The po

item.title: Advent to Hire Morgan Stanley to Sell Its Tigre Stake
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Advent hired Morgan Stanley to explore a potential sale of its stake in Tigre SA, a Brazilian manufacturer of pipe and hydraulic systems, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: How Afrobeats Became Nigeria’s ‘Winning Team’ At 2026 FIFA World Cup
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Globally, Afrobeats has shattered barriers, with streams surging on entertainment platforms and collaborations with Western and Latin stars becoming routine. The post How Afrobeats Became Nigeria’s ‘Winning Team’ At 2026 FIFA World Cup appeared first on Channels Television.
